meta.title: Is Scripture the Only Infallible Rule?
meta.author: Gavin Ortlund (with Jordan Cooper & Michael Horton)

questions.question: According to Jordan Cooper, sola scriptura is primarily about what?
options: Whether Christians may read tradition at all
Which authority is the primary infallible authority guiding the church's theology and practice
Whether the Bible is historically interesting
Whether pastors should preach in Latin
questions.answer: Which authority is the primary infallible authority guiding the church's theology and practice

questions.question: The speakers describe which view as a common misrepresentation of sola scriptura?
options: That the church should have councils
That theology begins from a blank slate: you alone read the Bible and pick a church that matches your opinion
That the Old Testament is Scripture
questions.answer: That theology begins from a blank slate: you alone read the Bible and pick a church that matches your opinion

questions.question: Cooper notes that sola in sola scriptura is which Latin grammatical construction, emphasizing 'by Scripture alone'?
questions.answer: Ablative

questions.question: Who is cited for distinguishing multiple senses in which 'tradition' can be used (e.g., in examining Trent)?
questions.answer: Martin Chemnitz

questions.question: When Roman Catholic authority speaks of 'tradition' in a binding sense, the discussion claims it does NOT chiefly mean what?
options: Tradition as church practice over centuries
Taking a statistical majority view among the church fathers on a doctrine
questions.answer: Taking a statistical majority view among the church fathers on a doctrine

questions.question: Calvin is quoted as grouping the Pope with which other movement as both threatening to 'bury' the word of God?
questions.answer: Anabaptists

questions.question: Michael Horton reads from the Catholic Catechism: Holy Tradition transmits what, according to the quoted section?
options: Only oral legends unrelated to Christ
In its entirety the Word of God entrusted to the apostles
questions.answer: In its entirety the Word of God entrusted to the apostles

questions.question: The speakers argue New Testament passages about 'tradition' delivered by the apostles often refer to what?
options: Apostolic teaching, including teaching first given orally
questions.answer: Apostolic teaching, including teaching first given orally

questions.question: Pope Benedict is cited as telling cardinals that some de fide doctrines are not in Scripture, implying what about dialogue over sola scriptura?
options: That sola scriptura is obviously true
That there is little point discussing sola scriptura if such doctrines must be accepted
That all dogmas are purely biblical
That the Old Testament is unnecessary
questions.answer: That there is little point discussing sola scriptura if such doctrines must be accepted

questions.question: Gregory the Great is cited reacting negatively to what title applied to himself?
questions.answer: Universal bishop / universal pontiff

questions.question: Which patristic figure is cited for preferring his view to be overturned if another proves a better case from Scripture?
questions.answer: Origen

questions.question: Regarding Scripture and tradition, what ontological claim do the speakers make?
options: Tradition and Scripture are identical in every respect
Tradition cannot ever mediate any truth
Tradition is not the Word of God in the same way Scripture is
questions.answer: Tradition is not the Word of God in the same way Scripture is

questions.question: The Second Helvetic Confession is used analogously to say the preached word is God's word how?
options: As new revelation equal to the apostles' writings
As a means of grace normed by Scripture, not as equally God-breathed as Scripture
questions.answer: As a means of grace normed by Scripture, not as equally God-breathed as Scripture

questions.question: On Mary, the discussion notes that positions such as the immaculate conception were once what for some Reformers, compared to later Roman requirements?
options: Optional or held variously, later becoming binding dogma
Always denied by every Reformer from birth
Defined only by Eastern Orthodox councils
questions.answer: Optional or held variously, later becoming binding dogma

questions.question: Josephus is cited regarding the Hebrew Scriptures to support what historical claim?
options: A council at Jamnia fixed the canon in 90 AD with infallibility
The Sadducees had the only true canon
There was no council that decided the Hebrew scriptures in the way the objection sometimes imagines
The New Testament quotes the Apocrypha as authoritative everywhere
questions.answer: There was no council that decided the Hebrew scriptures in the way the objection sometimes imagines

questions.question: Which early source is mentioned for the principle that only canonical scriptures were read publicly in church?
questions.answer: The Muratorian fragment

questions.question: Jordan Cooper's Jesus/Pharisees analogy aims to show what?
options: That the Pharisees had no canon
That Jesus rejected the Old Testament
That Jesus could affirm which writings were Scripture while still criticizing Pharisaic traditions
That the Sadducees were always right
questions.answer: That Jesus could affirm which writings were Scripture while still criticizing Pharisaic traditions

questions.question: A major criticism of the 'development of doctrine' hypothesis in the discussion is that it can become what?
options: Too focused on the Nicene Creed only
Amorphous and difficult to falsify when any critique is answered by 'development'
Identical to the Protestant view of unchanged tradition
questions.answer: Amorphous and difficult to falsify when any critique is answered by 'development'

questions.question: Which work is named as an example of prayer language about Mary that the speakers argue is not parallel to asking a friend for prayer?
options: Alphonsus Liguori's The Glories of Mary
questions.answer: Alphonsus Liguori's The Glories of Mary

questions.question: Michael Horton frames the magisterial Reformers' deepest concern about extra scriptural binding authority as touching what doctrine?
options: Christ's sole mediation and justification by grace alone through faith alone
questions.answer: Christ's sole mediation and justification by grace alone through faith alone

questions.question: The opening hook lists which examples of Roman Catholic dogmas said to be obligatory yet questioned as apostolic on biblical/patristic grounds?
options: Only clerical celibacy rules in the 1980s
Mariology (e.g., immaculate conception, assumption), papacy, papal infallibility
questions.answer: Mariology (e.g., immaculate conception, assumption), papacy, papal infallibility

questions.question: John Chrysostom is cited urging believers to test matters by Scripture and not follow teachers merely because of what?
options: Their personal charisma apart from following Scripture
questions.answer: Their personal charisma apart from following Scripture

questions.question: Chemnitz is used to insist that tradition about the canon of books is not identical to what?
options: Tradition as whatever the living magisterium teaches today
questions.answer: Tradition as whatever the living magisterium teaches today

questions.question: Tradition's inconsistency on which issue is used as an example of how tradition does not behave like fixed written Scripture?
options: Capital punishment (long consensus then sharp change in magisterial teaching)
questions.answer: Capital punishment (long consensus then sharp change in magisterial teaching)

questions.question: What is the stated purpose of the Truth Units series hosting this conversation?
options: To critique the existence of God
To avoid all discussion of Scripture
To respond to criticisms of the Protestant Reformation and defend its goods
questions.answer: To respond to criticisms of the Protestant Reformation and defend its goods
